model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Move the compact CNN toward the higher-capacity, better-optimized configuration that has been promising in prior CIFAR-10 runs: a slightly deeper/wider feature stack, a larger classifier, AdamW, a higher learning rate, smaller weight decay, schedule usage, and a larger batch size. This is a simple, coherent first candidate and keeps the script contract intact.","secondary_modes":["indexing","summaries","topk"]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"Reduce the step size from the current over-aggressive AdamW setting. The larger model helped, but the prior schedule-only trial regressed, so the next simplest move is to make optimization less noisy while keeping the architecture and schedule unchanged.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"Restore the learning rate that produced the best visible result so far. The schedule is already back on the longer horizon, so the most likely remaining gain is to undo the conservative step-size drop.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"Try a modestly more aggressive step size while keeping the model and schedule fixed. The current configuration matches the best visible result, so the next low-risk probe is a small upward nudge in learning rate rather than another structural change.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"The last step improved when the learning rate moved up, so continue that same direction with a slightly more aggressive but still plausible value. This is the cheapest next probe and keeps the rest of the configuration unchanged.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"Keep the current model and schedule, and push the step size a bit higher. The last increase still improved validation loss, so the next cheapest probe is a further upward adjustment in the same direction.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Trim regularization slightly now that the optimizer is already strong and the run still looks more optimization-limited than overfit-limited. A small weight-decay reduction is a low-risk way to test whether the model can fit a bit harder under the same step budget.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The run is still improving as regularization is reduced, and the current weight decay is already very small. Removing it entirely is the next minimal probe to see whether the model is still slightly underfit rather than overfit-limited.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"The last regularization tweak was a wash, so the remaining lever that has consistently moved the metric is learning-rate scale. A modest upward bump should test whether the current setup still has room to improve before the step budget runs out.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The optimization knobs have started to plateau and the model still looks somewhat underfit. A small width increase is the next simple way to add capacity without changing the training loop or schedule.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The width increase clearly regressed validation loss, while the earlier narrower model reached the best score so far. Revert the capacity bump and keep the current optimizer settings so the next run isolates the more promising baseline architecture.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"The last learning-rate increase was too aggressive and pushed loss up. Roll back to the last stable step size first so the run can recover toward the previously best configuration before trying any other change.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"The current setup is back at the best observed loss, so the next move should be a fine step-size refinement rather than a structural change. A slightly lower learning rate may smooth the late-stage updates and recover a small additional gain without altering the rest of the run.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"Restore the stable learning rate and spend the next probe on the cheapest remaining capacity increase, the classifier head. The convolutional stack is already near the best visible setting, so a slightly wider MLP head is the lowest-complexity way to test for residual underfitting.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The last widening of the classifier head improved validation loss, so the safest remaining capacity probe is to widen that head once more while keeping the convolutional trunk and optimizer unchanged. This keeps the change localized and tests whether the model is still bottlenecked at the final projection stage.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 160","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The wider classifier head hurt validation, and the best visible run used the narrower 128-unit head. Reverting that last capacity increase is the most direct way to recover the stronger configuration before spending the last steps on a new probe.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 160","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["layout"],"rationale":"The current model is back at the best known capacity setting, so the remaining unexplored knob is a tiny amount of regularization. Reintroducing a very small weight decay may improve validation without disturbing the stable optimizer setup.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["indexing"],"rationale":"The run has converged to the best known setting, and the only remaining low-risk probe is a very small learning-rate refinement around the current optimum. A slight step-size reduction may squeeze out a bit more stability without changing the model shape.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.9e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}
